fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Ohio Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Ohio — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license ohio, dental assistant certification ohio, RDA ohio, DANB CDA ohio, dental assistant radiography ohio

board.name: Ohio State Dental Board
board.mailing_address: 77 S. High St., 17th Floor, Columbus, OH 43215-6135

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 12 hours per renewal cycle (if radiography qualified)

license_types.title: Certified Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Ohio.

steps.title: Graduate from an Approved EFDA Program
steps.description: Complete a comprehensive Expanded Function Dental Auxiliary (EFDA) program that is approved by the Ohio State Dental Board.

steps.title: Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Successfully pass the EFDA clinical examination administered by the Commission on Dental Competency Assessments (CDCA) or another board-approved testing agency. Additionally, pass the Ohio State Dental Board Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Submit Application via eLicense Ohio
steps.description: Create an account on the eLicense Ohio portal and submit your initial EFDA registration application. Ensure all required documentation, including transcripts and exam scores, are uploaded or sent as instructed.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Records Check
steps.description: Submit fingerprints for a state and federal criminal records check. Detailed instructions for this process are typically provided during your application on the eLicense Ohio portal.

steps.title: Board Review and Registration Issuance
steps.description: The Ohio State Dental Board will review your complete application and all submitted materials. Upon successful verification of all requirements, your EFDA registration will be issued via eLicense Ohio.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Certified Dental Assistant through the Ohio State Dental Board by certification.

faqs.question: Does Ohio require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ohio requires Certified Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Ohio?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Ohio.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Ohio?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Ohio?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $45. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Certified Dental Assistant through the Ohio State Dental Board by certification.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Ohio
hero.dek: Ohio does not license general Dental Assistants. However, if you wish to perform expanded functions as an Expanded Function Dental Auxiliary (EFDA), you must register through the eLicense Ohio portal.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the eLicense Ohio homepage, click 'New User/Register Now', and follow the prompts to create a new user account with a User ID and password. Once registered, log in to start your application.
